title: nyssa sylvatica var. biflora
intro: Nyssa sylvatica var. biflora, also known as the two-flowered tupelo or two-flowered blackgum, is a variant of the blackgum tree (Nyssa sylvatica) that is native to the southeastern United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, as well as being used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Nyssa sylvatica var. biflora include a tea made from the bark and leaves, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions and internally to relieve respiratory issues.
